title: 三只瞎老鼠
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\151-三只瞎老鼠-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Mr. Bohns hosts a dinner for his guests in a private second-floor room at the George King Hotel, overlooking the harbor square. The attendees include Madame Maloway, Sir George, the talkative American Samuel Lathen, his clever daughter Yvonne, the preoccupied writer Evan Llewellyn, the intelligent Janet Lathamington, and Mr. Bohns's former partner, Leopold.

During the meal, Yvonne asks Mr. Bohns about a large diamond he had shown the group the previous night. Mr. Bohns identifies the gem as his "Morning Star," a talisman he keeps in a special inner pocket. Yvonne challenges him to a bet, claiming she has a plan to steal it. They agree on a wager: if she succeeds, Mr. Bohns must provide her with six pairs of high-quality nylon stockings, while she bets a new cigarette case. The guests discuss the stone's value, with Mr. Bohns noting it could be worth at least £30,000 even if it were re-cut.

To facilitate a demonstration, Mr. Bohns produces the diamond and passes it among the guests, including Madame Malo, Mr. Lechner, Llewellyn, Mr. Reuelin, and Leo Stein. Suddenly, Yvonne exclaims that the diamond has been dropped. As she reaches under the table to find it, a chaotic scene ensues; Sir George, Stein, Reuelin, Lady Ratherton, and Mrs. Maloway all bend down to assist her, causing a glass to fall. Amidst the confusion, Mr. Bohns remains seated, watching the scramble with a cold smile while sipping his wine.
